executive_summary: In highlight coverage, data from our scams chapter of the Crypto Crime Report was featured in Coindesk's weekly newsletter. Our research on illicit finance gained significant traction across several media: Crypto Briefing featured our Q1 data on $32M in stablecoin payments to gray-market peptide vendors, CoinMarketCap published our report on Chinese networks handling 20% of crypto laundering, and multiple FinanceFeeds articles cited our compliance tools analysis post-MiCA implementation across Korean, German, and Japanese markets. In crypto news, three major narratives dominated: BitMEX's shutdown signals regulatory consolidation in derivatives (CoinDesk), BNY's 24/7 tokenized Treasury settlement initiative accelerates institutional on-chain adoption (Bloomberg, CoinDesk), and the DOJ's Criminal Division raised concerns about crypto legislation's impact on money laundering prosecutions, with Senator Lummis warning that CLARITY Act delays threaten custody safety (Crypto Briefing, TradingView). In competitor news, TRM Labs generated 5 placements tracking HTX's wallet rotation to evade UK sanctions (Decrypt, The Block, Yahoo Finance), Elliptic announced a next-generation continuous monitoring solution and partnered with Zama on confidential finance compliance (HackerNoon), and Arkham Intelligence integrated Claude for memecoin analysis while identifying Robinhood's $5.84B crypto holdings (Crypto Briefing, CoinMarketCap).

narratives.title: Stablecoins Enable Gray-Market Commerce
narratives.description: Stablecoins surpassing Bitcoin as payment method for illicit peptide vendors, representing $32M in Q1 transactions.
narratives.relevance: Our research demonstrates stablecoins' real-world illicit adoption patterns, positioning us as authoritative source on emerging payment method risks for compliance teams.

market_landscape.title: Regulatory Clarity Stalls
market_landscape.body: CLARITY Act Senate delays amid DOJ concerns over prosecution impact signal ongoing legislative uncertainty despite crypto industry lobbying. Sentiment shifted from optimism to 38% passage probability, creating risk for compliance infrastructure investment decisions.

market_landscape.title: Institutional Onboarding Accelerates
market_landscape.body: BNY's 24/7 Treasury tokenization, Robinhood's prediction market ambitions, and Visa's stablecoin platform signal coordinated TradFi convergence. Weekend settlement elimination and institutional wallet identification (Arkham/Robinhood) reduce friction for capital deployment on-chain.

market_landscape.title: Illicit Finance Sophistication Deepens
market_landscape.body: HTX wallet rotation tactics, Chinese network dominance (20% laundering share), and stablecoin-based gray market payments demonstrate adaptation to surveillance. Compliance vendors face escalating sophistication in evasion techniques requiring continuous tool evolution.

competitor_reads.Elliptic: 💡 💡 Elliptic is positioning as Japan-focused compliance expert through WebX Tokyo participation and advancing continuous monitoring capabilities, while partnering with Zama on confidential finance compliance—signaling depth in emerging privacy-preserving use cases.
competitor_reads.TRM Labs: 💡 💡 TRM Labs generated 5 concurrent placements documenting HTX wallet rotation tactics to evade UK sanctions, establishing authority on sanctions evasion detection but risking reputational exposure if claims face pushback from HTX.
competitor_reads.Arkham Intelligence: 💡 💡 Arkham is expanding into AI-driven analysis via Claude integration for memecoin assessment and generating major holder visibility narratives (Robinhood's $5.84B portfolio), positioning as on-chain research platform rather than compliance-first vendor.
